arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner not looking beyond Aston Villa after celebrating a year at Crystal Palace
arts.sum: The current sequence of results came after Glasner and his side failed to win any of their opening eight top-flight fixtures – their worst-ever start to a Premier League campaign, itself coming off of an end-of-season run the previous campaign which saw the Eagles win six of their final seven games, scoring 21 goals, including the season-ending 5-0 victory against Aston Villa. After celebrating a year in the job last week, Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner is currently enjoying a run of form which has seen his side draw six and win seven of their last 17 Premier League games following their opening league win of the season against Spurs in late October.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace attacker to miss Aston Villa Premier League clash
arts.sum: With 60 minutes played for the U21s last week and a few minutes as a late substitute in the FA Cup win at Doncaster earlier this month, hopes were high that the 20-year-old would be back to full fitness soon, but a busy fixture period of three games in seven days means the opportunity for the young South American to train fully have been limited. Due to the Eagles’ clash with Newcastle United next month being postponed thanks to the league cup exploits of the north-east side, plus a scheduled international break, over three weeks separate the Ipswich game (March 8) to Palace’ next scheduled game, against Southampton (April 2) – although if the Eagles beat Millwall in the last 16 of the FA Cup this Saturday, they will move into the quarter-finals, scheduled for March 29.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa XI vs Crystal Palace – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Aston Villa head to Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Tuesday evening. Unai Emery’s side boosted their Champions League hopes with a 2-1 win over Chelsea at the weekend. Marco Asensio scored his first goals for the club with a brace, following his loan arrival from Paris Saint-Germain. Asensio’s double was assisted by Marcus […] The post Aston Villa XI vs Crystal Palace – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace to jet off to Marbella on mid-season training camp
arts.sum: Whatever the length of the break without a competitive game, the trip to Marbella is a carbon copy of the Eagles’ trip to Spain last year, where Glasner’s side faced Norwegian champions Bodo/Glimt in a friendly following the postponement of their clash against Newcastle due to their FA Cup progress. Due to the Eagles’ away clash with Newcastle United – originally scheduled for 15 March – being postponed to 16 April, thanks to the Carabao Cup exploits of the north-east side, plus a scheduled international break (17-25 March), over three weeks separate their Premier League game against Ipswich (8 March) to their next, scheduled game, against Southampton (2 April).

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace vs Aston Villa: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Aston Villa travel to Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Tuesday evening. Preview includes team news, predictions and how to watch on TV.

arts.ttl: Glasner on connecting with fans & the joy of football
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner spoke of the positivity surrounding Crystal Palace’s training ground after the weekend’s win over Fulham, and ahead of a challenging fixture against Aston Villa at Selhurst Park.

arts.ttl: Report: Mustapha blasts Palace past Baggies
arts.sum: Crystal Palace Under-21s made it three wins in a row with a comfortable win away against West Bromwich Albion at the Armco Arena. Second-half goals from Ademola Ola-Adebomi and Hindolo Mustapha saw the Eagles come away with all three points.

arts.ttl: Team News: Crystal Palace vs. Aston Villa injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Tuesday's Premier League clash between Crystal Palace and Aston Villa.

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner talks through home and away form difference as Aston Villa head to Crystal Palace
arts.sum: But Glasner is quick to distance himself from any link to the last time a midweek Premier League game was played at Selhurst – the 4-0 win at home on Monday Night Football against Manchester United last season – despite that recent run of away results. If we just show 90% of our level, and they play on their top level, we will lose and then it doesn’t matter if it’s an away or home game.

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner hints key man could return as Crystal Palace face Aston Villa
arts.sum: Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner hinted midfielder Adam Wharton could make his first Premier League start since October when the Eagles host Aston Villa . Palace beat Fulham 2-0 on Saturday, the first time in their history they have secured four consecutive top-flight victories on the road, but they have struggled at Selhurst Park where they have won just twice in the Premier League this season.
